item.title: The book you would read over and over again
item.description: Mine is Jim Butcher's Codex Alera series. It is just soul comfort. The audiobook version is also so very enjoyable. What's yours? Edit: Oop, this one blew up ^^ Thank you all for your lovely comments (/recommendations)!

item.title: screaming into the void? yelling for help?
item.description: Throw away account. Half screaming into the void, half asking for help. I am a MA resident, and as the meme says, I'm voting for the first time in my life. I have questions about my mail-in ballot that I can't find satisfactory answers to on google, and I am the only one in my family who isn't a MAGAt. If there are any fellow blue MA residents out there, I'd really appreciate a dm so I can throw a few questions at you. I do not want this to get to the FP, I don't care about points, I'm just looking for a nudge in the right direction.
item.dc:creator: randomkeyboardsmash

greetings online friends. I like doing minis but haven’t done one in a while, so here goes
impressive knife there
that must suuuuuck
that’s a cool looking creature/shell
mmm hmm
yeah that is pretty metal
kinda need to know where I can get one
fascinating
ding ding ding
as is tradition
i guess
that's my set.. have a great night gang!!

item.title: Dr. Alan Hart TB Pioneer AND Trans Pioneer.
item.description: Full Sauce: https://www.instagram.com/reel/C0scA7VN01Z/?igsh=OWVmbzNnZHpwa3By Extra sauce: https://connecticuthistory.org/alan-l-hart-pioneer-in-medicine-and-transgender-history/ 'Alan L. Hart was a 20th-century physician, radiologist, and author who pioneered the use of x-ray in early detection for tuberculosis. He spent the latter part of his career in Connecticut working for the state’s Tuberculosis Commission to expand public health screenings and services. Hart was also one of the first people to undergo gender affirmation surgery in the United States.' Mor sauce: https://www.opb.org/article/2022/06/30/oregon-us-gender-affirming-surgery-history-dr-alan-hart-lgbtqia-history/ 'It was 105 years ago that an Oregon doctor underwent what is thought to be the first documented gender-confirming surgery in the United States. Dr. Alan Hart was born Alberta “Lucille” Hart in 1890. Hart grew up in the Willamette Valley and attended Albany College, now Lewis & Clark College. Hart preferred to present as male and did so as much as possible – often with family support. But at school, Hart was forced to wear dresses and appear as “Lucille.”
item.dc:creator: HorrorMango

item.title: Sounds like a cool job!
item.dc:creator: countervail247365

item.title: Assholes can park after all…
item.description:
So; a while ago I found this council sticker in it’s bag, blown on the wind. Car authorised for removal sticker. I’ll be honest… I kept it because I thought I would prank a friend…
Several days later I find this asshole parking across two spots, and not for the first time (distinct car). It seemed the perfect opportunity? For the record, I was late for a meeting. Trying to find parking the previous time so I already had a petty vendetta.
I’m not sure this counts as ‘impersonating a police officer’ but I did fill out his car details with a fake ‘authorised for removal’ sticker for ‘Parking’. Did I do wrong? Obviously - *obviously* there is no chance how car would be touched, from the top floor of a car park; nobody else knew about it..?!
But I did feel quite happy leaving them this gift.
And who should I find in the same space today? But look how well they have parked now ?
Look at that consideration? Space for another car next to them? Isn’t that kind. So. I plead my guilt to the Imgur jury, and ask… was I right or was I wrong? Judge me!
item.dc:creator: GiddyKipper

item.title: Meme Dump 13 out of 200
item.description:
And it's another day of cleaning out my 11k meme folder from my phone. Here we go on the shenanigan adventure from 2017 to current
Can never unsee white Jay Z
Where I live its like this for months - I hate iiiiiit
And being a tall... this was the worst
Saw somewhere that you should start saying bless you at the same volume that they sneeze at
Say it like Shrek
Thanks for joining me ~ hope your Monday has been calm, and if it hasn't - treat yourself to something tastey ! See you tomorrow ♡

item.title: Some companies are just shit
item.description: She's in Florida, company is in Texas. Suing their asses for ADA discrimination is probably impractical and would cost her more emotional labor at this point than it would be worth, even if she could find an attorney who would work on contingency. She'll apply for unemployment and keep on looking for something better, but fuck companies like this. In the ass. With a sharp, dry cactus. MV Edit: Grateful for the support and advice. Will, as the legal eagles say, "take it under advisement" and discuss the matter with our daughter. Please vote Blue forever, everywhere; in the long run, this will strengthen workers' rights and unionization which exists to counter this kind of corporate bullshit. To those saying she was just a bad employee and the two things are not related, the chain of events is undisputed. All was syncopetic¹ until she told her supervisor that she had occasional seizures. She was immediately locked out of system access and told to wait for a company decision. It came this morning, and she was instructed to return her company computer at once. Up until then, things had been going well. ¹ Shit. "Copacetic." I *knew* that looked wrong, but I just couldn't dredge up the right word. *weeps in 72*
